purpose: Seed eval definition for the contract-first authoring boundary of skill-forge.

comparison_intent.hypothesis: With the skill enabled, responses should handle new-skill authoring, existing-skill refactors, and authoring clarification boundaries more reliably, while stopping at Eval Brief ready for trigger cases.

golden.prompt: Create a new reusable skill for spotting JavaScript array mutation mistakes. Start from the contract, keep one job only, and do not implement eval runtime behavior.
golden.expected_output: Treat the request as a new-skill authoring workflow, freeze the contract before writing instructions, and stop at Eval Brief ready.
assertions: must classify this as a new-skill workflow
must define the contract before final skill instructions
must stop before eval runtime implementation

golden.prompt: Refactor the existing skill `skill-eval-forge`. First map what it does now, compare it to the blueprint, then produce the correct boundary artifact without implementing runtime behavior.
golden.expected_output: Treat the request as an existing-skill refactor workflow, keep the work contract-first, and end with Eval Brief ready.
assertions: must classify this as an existing-skill refactor workflow
must preserve the contract-first refactor sequence
must end at Eval Brief ready

golden.prompt: Rewrite the existing skill `skill-eval-forge` using the current repo docs as the source of truth. Freeze the job, trigger boundary, nearby negatives, and success model before you touch the final instructions.
golden.expected_output: Treat the request as a skill-rewrite workflow, anchor the rewrite in source-backed contract work, and stop at Eval Brief ready.
assertions: must classify this as a skill rewrite request
must freeze contract elements before final instructions
must stop at Eval Brief ready

golden.prompt: Write a new skill for PR review routing and also set up the first eval scaffold for it. I want the skill contract first, but please leave the runtime for later.
golden.expected_output: Handle the authoring part, call out that eval work is downstream, and stop at Eval Brief ready instead of implementing the scaffold.
assertions: must preserve skill authoring as the active responsibility
must explicitly defer eval scaffold implementation
must not invent runner, grading, or benchmark behavior

negative.prompt: Update the repo AGENTS.md managed block and tighten the repository policy wording.
negative.expected_output: Do not trigger skill-forge because this is repository policy work, not skill authoring.
assertions: must classify AGENTS work as out of scope
must avoid rewriting the task as a skill request
must avoid producing an Eval Brief

negative.prompt: Build the offline eval runner for skill-forge with grading, timing, and benchmark output. Use Node, TypeScript, Zod, and AI SDK.
negative.expected_output: Do not trigger skill-forge because the request is shared eval runtime implementation, not skill authoring.
assertions: must classify runtime implementation as out of scope
must avoid proposing authoring-only output as if it satisfied the request
must avoid producing an Eval Brief

negative.prompt: Take this finished Eval Brief and turn it into golden and negative cases in packs/core/my-skill/evals/evals.json.
negative.expected_output: Do not trigger skill-forge because this is eval authoring work that belongs downstream.
assertions: must classify eval definition work as out of scope for skill-forge
must avoid redefining the task as skill authoring
must avoid producing an Eval Brief

negative.prompt: Create a skill, reorganize the repo policies, and build the first runner in one pass so everything lands together.
negative.expected_output: Do not trigger skill-forge as a clean fit because the request mixes multiple independent workflows and should stop for scope clarification.
assertions: must detect multiple workflows in one request
must refuse to collapse policy, authoring, and runtime into one skill flow
must avoid producing an Eval Brief before the scope is narrowed
